name: Pico Mencilla by Pineda de la Sierra — circular through the Sierra de Mancilla
name_original: Pico Mencilla por Pineda de la Sierra — circular por la Sierra de Mancilla
region_intro: Hiking around Pineda De La Sierra offers access to a diverse mountainous landscape within the Sierra de la Demanda. The region features prominent peaks such as San Millán (2,131 meters), Trigaza (2,085 meters), and Mencilla (1,932 meters). Lush native forests, including beech, oak, and pine woods, cover much of the terrain, while the Arlanzón River and its reservoirs contribute to the area's natural features. Best hiking trails around Pineda De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Pico Mencilla by Pineda de la Sierra — circular through the Sierra de Mancilla, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the Sierra de Mancilla. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Mencilla loop from Valle del Sol, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail provides a varied ascent to Pico Mencilla from Valle del Sol. Local hikers also love the Pineda de la Sierra – San Millán (2,131 m) loop from Pineda de la Sierra, a 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain towards San Millán, often completed in about 6 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Pineda De La Sierra is defined by mountainous terrain, native forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents of high peaks. The routes in Pineda De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Pineda De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: Pineda de la Sierra offers a diverse network of over 10 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents of high peaks within the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Pineda de la Sierra?
A: The terrain around Pineda de la Sierra is predominantly mountainous, featuring prominent peaks like San Millán, Trigaza, and Mencilla. You'll encounter lush native forests, including beech, oak, and pine woods, as well as picturesque riparian landscapes along the Arlanzón River and its reservoirs. Some routes to peaks like Mencilla may have rocky sections.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in the area?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are options suitable for families or those seeking easier walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, an old railway line, offers a gentler path through forests and along the Arlanzón Reservoir, ideal for a more relaxed experience. The region has 2 easy routes available.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: Yes, Pineda de la Sierra is generally dog-friendly. Many trails are suitable for hiking with dogs, allowing you to explore the natural beauty together. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with wildlife, and check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while hiking?
A: Hikers can enjoy stunning panoramic views from peaks like San Millán and Mencilla. The region also features the Arlanzón and Úzquiza reservoirs, offering scenic waterside paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2015001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt; provides a beautiful section through oak and beech forests. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1754887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;beautiful views over the Arlanzón reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Pineda de la Sierra offers several circular hiking routes. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041647715/pico-mencilla-by-pineda-de-la-sierra-circular-through-the-sierra-de-mancilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mencilla by Pineda de la Sierra — circular through the Sierra de Mancilla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de los Corros – Pico Mencilla loop from Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: The region is beautiful year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors in the native forests. Summer is also suitable, though it can be warmer, especially at lower elevations. Winter hiking is possible, with opportunities for snowshoeing or skiing at the nearby Valle del Sol resort, but requires appropriate gear and caution.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Pineda de la Sierra, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. Specific trailheads, such as those leading to Pico Mencilla from Valle del Sol, also have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I explore near the hiking trails?
A: The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; itself is a cultural highlight, with medieval origins and well-preserved traditional sandstone houses. You can visit the Romanesque Church of San Esteban and the Church of Santa Juliana, both Assets of Cultural Interest, adding a historical dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: Are there options for challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Pineda de la Sierra is home to several challenging routes, including ascents to the highest peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40012091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pineda de la Sierra – San Millán (2,131 m) loop from Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding trail leading to the summit of San Millán, offering significant elevation gain and requiring good fitness. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m) – Pineda de la Sierra loop from Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is even longer.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: The hiking routes in Pineda de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the beauty of the native forests, and the rewarding views from the peaks.

Q: Are there any trails that follow water features like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. The Arlanzón River flows through the area, and the Arlanzón and Úzquiza reservoirs are significant natural landmarks. The GR-82 long-distance footpath includes segments that trace the Arlanzón Reservoir, offering continuous scenic views of the water.
tips: The climb up Pineda has some rocky and uncomfortable sections, but it is not difficult. You just have to be careful with the forecast of fog.
The descent to Tinieblas de la Sierra is a descent that goes towards the town of Pineda de la Sierra on the northern side of the Pico Mencilla mountain.

Pineda was an important center for nomadic cattle ranching during the Middle Ages. The urban complex preserves its mountain character between the San Millán massif and the Sierra de Mencilla, it has magnificent manor houses with red ashlars in sandstone from the sierra. The church of San Esteban preserves the original Romanesque-style apse and a beautiful arcaded gallery. The town is in the path of the Vía Verde de la Demanda and other great routes for hiking, cycling and active leisure.

waypoints.name: Descent to Tinieblas de la Sierra
